mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.2pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining inflation indicates strong consumer demand without pricing pressure, supporting corporate revenue growth and margin expansion, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and falling CPIAUCSL yields a negative or zero Rank IC over 60-day forward returns across multiple 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic activity and higher capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns during mid-cycle expansions, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds a critical threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320.0, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC ~ 0).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC against 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and real GDP signal broad-based economic strength and rising asset prices, which enhance financial sector profitability and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising HOUST and GDPC1 yields a negative Rank IC for 60-day forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates an economic contraction, which reduces corporate cash flows and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises simultaneously during recessions.
